Today is February 19, 2026. President Donald Trump recently spoke at the inaugural meeting of his Board of Peace. According to the reports, what was the original intended purpose of this board when it was first announced?
(A) To help end the war between Israel and Hamas in Gaza and oversee reconstruction
(B) To negotiate a new nuclear agreement with the Islamic Republic of Iran
(C) To replace the United Nations as the primary body for international conflict resolution
(D) To coordinate the surge of military forces and aircraft carriers to the Middle East
(E) To manage the diplomatic relations between the US, Israel, and Saudi Arabia
explanation: The article states that when Trump first announced the Board of Peace, "it was thought to be aimed at helping end the two-year war between Israel and Hamas in Gaza and oversee reconstruction."
model_output:
Based on recent reports from early 2026, the Board of Peace was first announced as part of President Trump's comprehensive plan to end the conflict in Gaza, with a focus on overseeing a ceasefire and subsequent reconstruction efforts there.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [White House] (https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefings-statements/2026/01/statement-on-president-trumps-comprehensive-plan-to-end-the-gaza-conflict)[[1]](https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefings-statements/2026/01/statement-on-president-trumps-comprehensive-plan-to-end-the-gaza-conflict)
- [NPR] (https://www.npr.org/2026/02/19/nx-s1-5719053/trump-board-of-peace-meeting)[[2]](https://www.npr.org/2026/02/19/nx-s1-5719053/trump-board-of-peace-meeting)

Today is February 19, 2026. Authorities have provided details regarding the victims of the avalanche in the Castle Peak area. What is the gender breakdown of the nine individuals confirmed or presumed dead?
(A) Seven women and two men
(B) Five women and four men
(C) Six women and three men
(D) Eight women and one man
(E) Four women and five men
explanation: The article states: "Officials say the victims are seven women and two men."
model_output:
Based on the search results, an avalanche occurred on February 17, 2026, in the Perry Peak area near Castle Peak, California. It involved a group of backcountry skiers (reported as 15 people total in some sources: nine women and six men). Six survived, while nine did not (eight confirmed dead and one missing/presumed dead). Authorities reported that of those nine, seven were women and two were men. This was noted as the deadliest avalanche in California's history.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is February 19, 2026. The singer Bad Bunny is set to star in the film "Porto Rico." Which historical figure is the film partly based on?
(A) René Pérez
(B) Javier Bardem
(C) José Maldonado Román
(D) Águila Negra
(E) Alejandro G Iñárritu
explanation: The article states: "The film... is partly based on the life of José Maldonado Román, a late 19th and early 20th Century revolutionary."
model_output:
The film "Porto Rico" is described in multiple sources as a historical drama inspired by true events from Puerto Rico's past, specifically drawing from the life of the revolutionary José Maldonado Román, also known as Águila Blanca.[[1]](https://deadline.com/2026/02/bad-bunny-porto-rico-movie-ed-norton-javier-bardem-residente-1236727219)[[2]](https://www.theguardian.com/music/2026/feb/18/bad-bunny-film-role-porto-rico-residente)[[3]](https://www.yahoo.com/entertainment/movies/articles/bad-bunny-lands-first-lead-120010267.html) He was a key figure in the fight against Spanish colonialism in the late 19th and early 20th centuries, including participation in uprisings like the Yauco Uprising.[[2]](https://www.theguardian.com/music/2026/feb/18/bad-bunny-film-role-porto-rico-residente) While the film blends historical elements with a narrative approach, this matches option (C) as the historical figure it's partly based on. Note that option (D) appears to be a close but incorrect variant (Águila Blanca means "White Eagle," not "Black Eagle").

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Deadline ([https://deadline.com/2026/02/bad-bunny-porto-rico-movie-ed-norton-javier-bardem-residente-1236727219])
- The Guardian ([https://www.theguardian.com/music/2026/feb/18/bad-bunny-film-role-porto-rico-residente])
- Yahoo Entertainment ([https://www.yahoo.com/entertainment/movies/articles/bad-bunny-lands-first-lead-120010267.html])

Today is February 19, 2026. The US is increasing its military presence near Iran. According to US officials, by when are all deployed forces expected to be in place in the region?
(A) Within the next three weeks
(B) By this Saturday
(C) By the end of February
(D) Within 10 days
(E) By mid-March
explanation: The article states: "All US military forces deployed to the region were expected to be in place by mid-March, a US official and a senior military official told CBS."
model_output:
Based on recent reports from US officials amid the escalating tensions with Iran, while some military assets are already in the region and preparations allow for potential strikes as early as this weekend, the full deployment of all forces is not expected until later. Specifically, multiple sources indicate that all deployed forces are expected to be in place by mid-March[[1]](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/trump-possible-timeline-iran-strikes)[[2]](https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/18/trump-iran-us-military-reports)[[3]](https://wbznewsradio.iheart.com/content/2026-02-19-us-sends-more-weaponry-into-the-middle-east-for-possible-strikes-on-iran)[[4]](https://english.aawsat.com/world/5242433-us-military-tells-trump-its-%E2%80%98ready%E2%80%99-strike-iran-soon-saturday)[[5]](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/2/18/white-house-says-iran-would-be-wise-to-take-deal-amid-military-buildup).

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is February 19, 2026. Following the death of Jesse Jackson, former POW Andrew Ramirez recalled Jackson's 1999 mission to Yugoslavia. Which US Representative (and later Governor) accompanied Jackson on that trip?
(A) Jesse Jackson Jr.
(B) David Axelrod
(C) Rod Blagojevich
(D) Andrew Ramirez
(E) Steven Gonzales
explanation: The article identifies the individual as "Illinois Representative - and later Governor - Rod Blagojevich."
model_output:
I recall that in 1999, during the Kosovo War, Jesse Jackson led a delegation to Yugoslavia (now Serbia) to negotiate the release of three captured US soldiers: Staff Sgt. Andrew Ramirez, Staff Sgt. Christopher Stone, and Spc. Steven Gonzales. They were held by Yugoslav forces under Slobodan Milosevic.

Based on historical reports, US Representative Rod Blagojevich (D-IL) was part of that delegation and accompanied Jackson on the trip. Blagojevich later became the Governor of Illinois in 2003. None of the other options fit the description of being a US Representative at the time who later became a Governor and was documented as accompanying Jackson on that specific mission.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [Baltimore Sun] (https://www.baltimoresun.com/1999/05/01/freedom-no-closer-for-pows-jesse-jackson-brings-3-us-soldiers-tapes-from-families-prayers-release-not-on-agenda-last-ditch-appeal-to-milosevic-today-war-in-yugoslavia)[[1]](https://www.baltimoresun.com/1999/05/01/freedom-no-closer-for-pows-jesse-jackson-brings-3-us-soldiers-tapes-from-families-prayers-release-not-on-agenda-last-ditch-appeal-to-milosevic-today-war-in-yugoslavia)

===============================================

Today is February 19, 2026. Jesse Jackson has passed away at the age of 84. What was his birth name?
(A) Jesse Louis Jackson
(B) Noah Robinson
(C) Charles Jackson
(D) Jesse Louis Burns
(E) Jesse Louis Robinson
explanation: The article states: "Jackson was born Jesse Louis Burns on 8 October 1941 in Greenville, South Carolina."
model_output:
Jesse Jackson, the civil rights leader, was born on October 8, 1941, in Greenville, South Carolina[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jesse_Jackson). His birth name was Jesse Louis Burns, taken from his mother Helen Burns, as he was born out of wedlock[[2]](https://www.biography.com/activists/jesse-jackson)[[3]](https://www.britannica.com/question/Why-did-Jesse-Jackson-change-his-name). He was later adopted by his stepfather, Charles Henry Jackson, and changed his surname to Jackson[[4]](https://www.thehistorymakers.org/biography/reverend-jesse-l-jackson). His biological father was Noah Louis Robinson, but that was not part of his name at birth[[5]](https://www.wyff4.com/article/jesse-jackson-bio-life-timeline/70390275). This rules out the other options, which either reflect his adopted name or mix in elements from his family history.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [Wikipedia] (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jesse_Jackson)
- [WYFF4] (https://www.wyff4.com/article/jesse-jackson-bio-life-timeline/70390275)
- [Biography.com] (https://www.biography.com/activists/jesse-jackson)
- [The HistoryMakers] (https://www.thehistorymakers.org/biography/reverend-jesse-l-jackson)
- [Britannica] (https://www.britannica.com/question/Why-did-Jesse-Jackson-change-his-name)

Today is February 19, 2026. The White House ballroom project has switched architects. Who is the current architect handling the project?
(A) Shalom Baranes
(B) James McCrery
(C) Rodney Mims Cook Jr
(D) Thomas Luebke
(E) Chamberlain Harris
explanation: The article states: "...the administration switched architects on the project to Shalom Baranes."
model_output:
Based on recent reports, the White House ballroom project initially had James McCrery as the lead architect[[1]](https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefings-statements/2025/07/the-white-house-announces-white-house-ballroom-construction-to-begin)[[2]](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/19/us/politics/trump-ballroom-fine-arts-commission.html). However, the project switched architects in December 2025, with Shalom Baranes (and his firm, Shalom Baranes Associates) taking over as the new lead architect[[3]](https://www.cnn.com/2026/02/14/politics/white-house-ballroom-rendering-east-wing)[[4]](https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/15/trump-white-house-ballroom-renderings). As of February 19, 2026, Baranes remains in that role and has been actively involved in presenting concepts and renderings to the Commission of Fine Arts[[5]](https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2026/02/19/trump-white-house-ballroom-fine-arts)[[6]](https://www.politico.com/news/2026/02/19/trumps-appointees-on-fine-arts-panel-ok-big-white-house-ballroom-00786568)[[7]](https://www.dezeen.com/2026/02/17/drawings-white-house-ballroom-baranes)[[8]](https://www.kulr8.com/news/arts-panel-made-up-of-trump-appointees-approves-his-white-house-ballroom-proposal/article_a246a6b3-680e-5228-80e2-40f8349ceb24.html). McCrery is now described as the former lead architect[[9]](https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/2026/02/19/commission-fine-arts-white-house-ballroom-trump-approval/88761398007). None of the reports indicate involvement from the other listed individuals as the current architect.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:

Today is February 19, 2026. The Sierra Avalanche Center rated the destructive potential of the avalanche that trapped skiers in California. What was the specific rating on the D1 to D5 scale?
(A) D1.5
(B) D2.5
(C) D3.0
(D) D5.0
(E) D2.0
explanation: The article states: "The avalanche that trapped the skiers was rated as a D2.5 on a destructive potential scale of D1 to D5."
model_output:
Based on recent news reports about the deadly avalanche near Castle Peak in California's Sierra Nevada Mountains (which occurred on February 17, 2026, and trapped multiple backcountry skiers), the Sierra Avalanche Center rated its destructive potential as D2.5 on the D1 to D5 scale.[[1]](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/19/us/avalanche-lake-tahoe-deaths.html)[[2]](https://www.rgj.com/story/news/2026/02/19/deadly-avalanche-near-truckee-and-lake-tahoe-what-we-know-thursday/88749342007)[[3]](https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cvg19dp4vxgo) This is described as having the potential to bury or destroy a car, damage a truck, or break trees, but it's not at the extreme end of the scale.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

Today is February 19, 2026. While the overall US trade deficit rose, the deficit with China saw a significant decrease. What was the US trade deficit with China last year?
(A) $1.2 trillion
(B) $3.4 trillion
(C) $901.5 billion
(D) $202.1 billion
(E) $30 billion
explanation: The article states: "US trade with China... fell to reduce the deficit by roughly 30% to $202.1bn."
model_output:
Based on the web search results from official sources like the U.S. Census Bureau and Bureau of Economic Analysis, as well as reports from reputable news outlets (e.g., AP News, The New York Times, Forbes), the U.S. trade deficit with China for the full year 2025 was reported as $202.1 billion. This represents a significant decrease (around 32%) from the previous year, aligning with the details provided in the query, while the overall U.S. trade deficit (particularly for goods) rose to a record level in some analyses, reaching approximately $1.2 trillion[[1]](https://www.washingtonpost.com/business/2026/02/19/tariffs-trade-deficit-2025)[[2]](https://www.bea.gov/news/2026/us-international-trade-goods-and-services-december-and-annual-2025)[[3]](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/19/us-trade-deficit-totaled-901-billion-in-2025-despite-trumps-tariffs.html)[[4]](https://www.forbes.com/sites/kenroberts/2026/02/19/new-data-2025-us-trade-set-record-at-559-trillion-despite-tariffs)[[5]](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c4ge4yxwnlno).

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- U.S. runs record trade deficit in goods, despite Trump's tariffs policy (https://www.washingtonpost.com/business/2026/02/19/tariffs-trade-deficit-2025)
- US trade deficit declined in 2025, but gap for goods hits a record despite Trump tariffs (https://apnews.com/article/trump-trade-deficit-tariffs-china-9eb6bd10ff635d63e46ee99d34ce1d05)
- U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services, December and Annual 2025 (https://www.bea.gov/news/2026/us-international-trade-goods-and-services-december-and-annual-2025)
- U.S. trade deficit totaled $901 billion in 2025, despite Trump tariffs (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/19/us-trade-deficit-totaled-901-billion-in-2025-despite-trumps-tariffs.html)
- 2025 U.S. Trade Reached $5.6 Trillion, A New Record, Despite Tariffs (https://www.forbes.com/sites/kenroberts/2026/02/19/new-data-2025-us-trade-set-record-at-559-trillion-despite-tariffs)
- US trade deficit hits fresh high despite Trump's tariffs (https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c4ge4yxwnlno)

Today is February 19, 2026. In his opening statement to lawmakers, Les Wexner addressed his visits to Jeffrey Epstein's private island. How long did he say he visited the island for?
(A) Several days
(B) One week
(C) Overnight
(D) A few hours
(E) He denied ever visiting the island
explanation: The article states: "Wexner also said he had visited Epstein's private island for 'a few hours' with his family."
model_output:
According to recent reports on Les Wexner's February 2026 deposition and statement to the House Oversight Committee regarding his ties to Jeffrey Epstein, Wexner acknowledged visiting Epstein's private island (Little St. James) only once. In his written opening statement, he specified that the visit was brief, occurring shortly after Epstein purchased the island, when Wexner, his wife Abigail, and their young children stopped there while on a family cruise.

The exact wording from his statement: "I visited Epstein’s island only once, shortly after he purchased it, when Abigail, our young children, and I stopped for a few hours one morning while we were on a cruise on our boat."[[1]](https://abc6onyourside.com/news/local/full-text-les-wexner-issues-3-page-statement-before-deposition-leslie-l-brands-limited-jeffrey-epstein-files-columbus-billionaire-ohio-new-albany-bexley)

This matches option (D). Wexner denied any wrongdoing or knowledge of Epstein's crimes but did not deny the visit itself.

CITATIONS:
- [ABC6] (https://abc6onyourside.com/news/local/full-text-les-wexner-issues-3-page-statement-before-deposition-leslie-l-brands-limited-jeffrey-epstein-files-columbus-billionaire-ohio-new-albany-bexley)
- [BBC] (https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cy57rrrpvg7o)
- [NBC News] (https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/congress/billionaire-les-wexner-tells-lawmakers-was-conned-jeffrey-epstein-noth-rcna259573)

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

Today is February 19, 2026. Jesse Jackson was famous for his powerful oratory. Which specific refrain from his 1988 Democratic National Convention speech was later echoed by Barack Obama's 2008 campaign?
(A) Hope and change
(B) Rainbow coalition
(C) Yankee go home
(D) I have a dream
(E) Keep hope alive
explanation: The article states: "His speech to the 1988 Democratic National Convention, which ended with the refrain 'keep hope alive', would be echoed decades later in the 'hope and change' slogan of Obama's successful 2008 presidential campaign."
model_output:
I recall that Jesse Jackson's 1988 DNC speech famously ended with the repeated refrain "Keep hope alive!" This theme of hope was a central pillar of Barack Obama's 2008 presidential campaign, including in his slogan "Hope and Change" and his book *The Audacity of Hope*. Sources confirm that Jackson's phrase was directly echoed in Obama's messaging.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [BBC](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cx77214zz9eo)[[1]](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cx77214zz9eo)
